# Lumi — Agentic AI Math Tutor for K–5

> Lumi is a voice-and-text AI math tutor for Kindergarten through Grade 5 (ages 5–10),
> built on Claude (Anthropic) with Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool-use. Math is
> verified by deterministic tools so the model can never present a wrong answer as
> correct; the LLM handles adaptive, grade-aware Socratic dialogue. The project ships
> with an LLM-as-judge evaluation suite, LangSmith observability, and a 10-dimension
> reliability assessment. It is a portfolio project demonstrating production-grade AI
> engineering for a child-facing product.

## Author

Twisha Desai — Staff Software Engineer. Focus: applied LLM engineering, agentic
tool-use, evaluation, guardrails, and reliability for AI products.

## What it demonstrates

- Guardrails for AI serving children: correctness is hard-enforced in code (deterministic
  answer-checking via a restricted AST evaluator), not left to the model.
- Agentic tool-use over MCP with a bounded loop (`_MAX_TOOL_ITERATIONS`) and a safe fallback.
- Evaluation discipline: deterministic evaluators plus an LLM-as-judge no-spoiler and
  tool-compliance check, run against LangSmith.
- Multi-tenant data isolation: per-session conversation state so concurrent students never
  share context.
- Production reliability thinking: a severity-ranked assessment across ten dimensions.

## Tech stack

Python; Claude via Anthropic API; Model Context Protocol (MCP); Streamlit; LangSmith
observability; OpenAI Whisper (speech-to-text); pyttsx3 (text-to-speech); SQLite.

## Key files

- README.md — overview, architecture diagram, setup, and design decisions.
- RELIABILITY_ASSESSMENT.md — 10-dimension reliability assessment with code evidence and
  severity-ranked remediation.
- core/tutor_brain.py — agentic loop (Claude API + LangSmith tracing + bounded tool-use).
- core/tools.py — grade-aware tool implementations (calculate, check_answer, generate_problem,
  check_topic, check_grade_level, classify_misconception).
- core/mcp_server.py / core/mcp_bridge.py — MCP server and client bridge.
- core/prompts.py — grade-specific Socratic system prompts.
- app.py — Streamlit UI with grade picker and per-session state.
- evals/ — golden cases, deterministic evaluators, LLM-as-judge, and LangSmith batch runner.

## Notes

Correctness is deterministic and hard-enforced. Grade/topic scoping is advisory (prompt- and
tool-informed), a documented design choice with a code-level enforcement path noted in the
reliability assessment.
